It is the largest in history: 48 teams, 104 matches, 39 days. He will leave at the end of June, according to the Washington Post. The question is not whether AI can do more, but whether […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> Swiss startup GR3N raises €15.5M to build the world’s first microwave-powered PET recycling plant https://thenextweb.com/news/gr3n-pet-recycling-series-b-microwave Sat, 06 Jun 2026 13:16:52 +0000 TheNextWeb=c952d223459c4e2d4ab131ac324c1131 Swiss cleantech startup GR3N has raised €15.5 million in a Series B round to build the world’s first commercial-scale microwave-assisted PET recycling plant. The round was led by 360 Capital, with new investor VP Textile also participating. The proceeds will fund MODUS, a 40,000-ton-per-year facility in Spain. PET is one of the most widely used […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> Self-replicating Miasma worm hits 73 Microsoft GitHub repositories in supply chain attack https://thenextweb.com/news/miasma-worm-microsoft-github-supply-chain Sat, 06 Jun 2026 12:46:50 +0000 TheNextWeb=b5a933342c38e3e0ec16c18de4b783a5 The self-replicating Miasma worm has reached Microsoft‘s own GitHub repositories. GitHub disabled 73 repositories across four Microsoft organisations, including Azure, Azure-Samples, Microsoft, and MicrosoftDocs, after the worm planted malicious code that harvests developer credentials. It is the most significant escalation yet in an ongoing supply chain attack campaign that has been spreading across the open-source […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> An AI agent found 21 zero-days in FFmpeg for $1,000. Chrome just patched a record 429 bugs. https://thenextweb.com/news/ai-agent-21-zero-days-ffmpeg-chrome-429 Sat, 06 Jun 2026 12:24:11 +0000 TheNextWeb=18557b12983ddd4c4a2506beeab3a05c A security startup’s autonomous AI agent found 21 previously unknown vulnerabilities in FFmpeg, the open-source media library embedded in almost everything that touches video. The startup, depthfirst, says the run cost roughly $1,000 in compute. Some of the bugs had been hiding in the codebase for more than 20 years. Days later, Google shipped Chrome […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> EU trade chief wants a new tool to break Europe’s dependence on Chinese chips and rare earths https://thenextweb.com/news/eu-sefcovic-diversification-instrument-china-supply-chain Sat, 06 Jun 2026 10:59:26 +0000 TheNextWeb=ff5baec80541405abd5487e7ee08fe16 EU trade commissioner Maroš Šefčovič has called for a new “diversification instrument“ to reduce Europe’s dependence on single suppliers of chips and rare earths. He made the proposal at the European Policy Center’s Brussels Economic Security Forum on Friday. The tool would force companies in sensitive sectors to source from at least three different suppliers. […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> Reid Hoffman leaves Microsoft’s board to go ‘founder mode’ with AI drug startup Manus https://thenextweb.com/news/reid-hoffman-leaves-microsoft-board-manus Sat, 06 Jun 2026 10:36:19 +0000 TheNextWeb=f1c621a775d5364fd238658437e46f11 Reid Hoffman is stepping down from Microsoft‘s board of directors after nearly a decade. Hoffman joined the board in 2016 after Microsoft bought his company LinkedIn for $26.2 billion. […] This story continues at The Next Web]]>
